nativeRange.habitat: Cultivar of hybrid origin not occurring in the wild; parent species inhabit shaded streams, riverbanks, and forest swamps in tropical West and Central Africa, where they grow epiphytically on submerged wood and rocks

decorPreferences: shaded areas under taller plants or floating cover

summary: A medium-sized hybrid Anubias cultivar (the name 'Nancon' contracts its parent names) with elongated, pointed dark green leaves; epiphytic, slow-growing, and undemanding, suited to the midground or background.
careNotes: Anubias 'Nancon' is a man-made cultivar widely attributed to aquarist Bob Gasser, created by crossing Anubias barteri var. 'Nana' with Anubias congensis (the latter sometimes labelled in trade as 'Anubias afzelii Congensis'; A. congensis is treated taxonomically as a synonym of A. heterophylla). The trade name 'Nancon' is a contraction of the two parent epithets. The hybrid is intermediate between its parents: more compact than A. congensis but taller and with longer, more lanceolate leaves than A. nana, with mature plants commonly reported in the 13–28 cm height range (Chichi's Planted Dreams ships sizes from roughly 5 to 11+ inches). Husbandry is the same as for other Anubias: the plant is epiphytic and the rhizome MUST remain above the substrate — attach it to driftwood, rocks, or other hardscape using cyanoacrylate glue, cotton thread, or fishing line, and bury only the white roots. Burying the rhizome causes rot and plant death. Lighting is generally low to medium; bright light combined with the cultivar's slow growth tends to promote algae on the leaves, so shading from floating or taller plants helps. CO2 injection is not required and Anubias are commonly reported as not responding strongly to it. Reported parameter tolerances are wide: temperature roughly 22–28 °C (72–82 °F) and pH roughly 5.5–9.0 across sources, with the central aquarium-safe envelope generally cited around 22–27 °C and pH 6.0–8.0. Hardness range is loosely reported but most sources agree the plant tolerates soft to moderately hard water. Feeds primarily from the water column, so liquid fertilization is more impactful than substrate fertilization. Cross-source notes: KeepFishKeeping records the parentage as A. barteri var. nana × A. heterophylla (taxonomically equivalent to nana × congensis), while Aquatic Arts, Wetplants, Betta Botanicals, and Chichi's Planted Dreams use the older trade label A. afzelii 'Congensis' for the second parent — these describe the same hybrid under different nomenclature. Buce Plant, Modern Aquarium, and Aquarium Co-Op product pages for this cultivar returned 404 at research time.
breedingNotes: Propagated by rhizome division: cut the rhizome with a clean blade into segments, each retaining several leaves and some healthy roots, and reattach the divisions to hardscape. Like other Anubias hybrids, the plant is generally sterile or seldom flowers in aquaria, so vegetative division is the practical home-aquarium method.

primary.name: KeepFishKeeping
primary.notes: Provided family (Araceae), parentage (A. barteri var. nana x A. heterophylla), lighting (low to medium), growth (slow to medium), care level (easy/beginner), placement (mid to background), spreading rhizome growth form, and the rhizome-must-not-be-buried husbandry warning.

additional.name: Aquatic Arts
additional.notes: Designated secondary (Buce Plant 404'd at the assigned URL). Cross-checked parentage (gives trade label 'A. barteri Nana x A. afzelii Congensis'), temperature 22-27C, pH 5.5-9.0, low-to-moderate lighting, no CO2 dependence, slow growth, midground-to-background placement, easy care, and the rhizome-attachment requirement. Flagged Panaque plecos as a known leaf-chewer exception.

additional.name: Chichi's Planted Dreams
additional.notes: Cross-checked sizing: ships Small (~5 in), Medium (~7 in), Large (~9 in), XL (11+ in), giving an approximate adult height range of 13-28 cm. Confirmed temperature 22-27C, pH 5.5-9.0, slow steady growth, low-to-moderate lighting, no CO2 dependence, midground-to-background placement, and the rhizome-not-buried rule.

additional.name: Wetplants
additional.notes: Cross-checked family (Araceae), East Tropical African parent origin, creeping/spreading rhizome growth form, pH 6-8, low-medium lighting, slow growth, easy care, and midground-to-background placement. Attributes the cultivar to aquarist Bob Gasser.

additional.name: Betta Botanicals
additional.notes: Cross-checked parentage (combining resilience of A. barteri 'Nana' with elongated leaf shape of A. afzelii 'Congensis'), low-to-medium lighting, no CO2 requirement, slow-to-medium growth via steady rhizome expansion, midground-to-background placement, propagation by rhizome division, easy care, and tolerance of a wide parameter range.
